Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Higher in AtMYB12 fruit than WT; elevated in peel where SlMYB12 is expressed
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis supplying precursors for shikimate/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary carbon metabolism linked to aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for shikimate pathway and phenylpropanoid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; chlorogenic acid and other phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of SlENO by AtMYB12 is associated with increased carbon flux through glycolysis and enhanced supply for phenylpropanoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point/linking primary metabolism to aromatic amino acid metabolism (as discussed in paper for ENO and DAHPS)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; promoter scanning; qRT-PCR in peel/flesh
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlENO transcript levels increased in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 directly bound the SlENO promoter. 3. SlENO is implicated in enhanced glycolytic flux that supports phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit at 3–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains AtMYB12/SlMYB12 binding motif TACCTACC.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Higher in AtMYB12 fruit than WT; elevated in peel where SlMYB12 is expressed
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP, the first step of the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for aromatic amino acids and downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; chlorogenic acid and other phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct activation of SlDAHPS by AtMYB12 increases shikimate-pathway precursor supply to support high phenylpropanoid production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of carbon flow into shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; promoter scanning; qRT-PCR in peel/flesh
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDAHPS expression was increased in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 directly bound the SlDAHPS promoter. 3. Activation of SlDAHPS is linked to enhanced aromatic amino acid biosynthesis that fuels phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit at 3–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly describes DAHPS as a key determinant of flow of carbon into shikimate pathway; promoter contains TACCTACC motif.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Higher in AtMYB12 fruit than WT; elevated in peel with SlMYB12 expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone precursor of flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early committed precursor for flavonols and anthocyanins; competes with stilbene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct activation of SlCHS1 by AtMYB12/SlMYB12 promotes flavonoid biosynthesis; competition by VvStSy can reduce flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; promoter scanning; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCHS1 promoter was significantly enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP and bound by SlMYB12 in WT peel. 2. SlCHS1 expression is elevated where MYB12 activity is present. 3. CHS branch flux can be competitively diverted by stilbene synthase, reducing flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 StSy fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit around breaker +3 to +7 days
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with VvStSy lowers high flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 StSy tomatoes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains conserved MYB12 binding motif TACCTACC.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in WT peel with SlMYB12 expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to form dihydroflavonols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for flavonols and anthocyanins; competes with isoflavone synthase for naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; anthocyanins; inverse relation to genistein when reduced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct target of AtMYB12 promoting flavonol biosynthesis; reduced F3H activity in are mutant redirects flux to isoflavones and lowers flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mutant analysis using are; metabolite profiling after genetic crosses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlF3H is a direct/known MYB12 target in tomato flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. Loss of F3H activity in the are mutant reduced flavonols to about 30% of AtMYB12 fruit levels. 3. Because F3H and IFS compete for naringenin, reduced F3H greatly increased isoflavone accumulation in AtMYB12 LjIFS backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit; genetic background containing are mutation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced F3H lowers flavonol accumulation while increasing isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: are is described as a naturally occurring mutant deficient in F3H activity.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Elevated in WT peel and associated with SlMYB12 expression; known AtMYB12 target from prior work cited
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with flavonol accumulation under MYB12 activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlFLS expression is elevated in tomato peel where SlMYB12 is expressed. 2. The paper notes FLS as a known target of AtMYB12 from previous studies. 3. Increased FLS expression is consistent with high flavonol accumulation in MYB12 backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: WT fruit peel and flesh at 5 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding was cited from prior work rather than newly demonstrated here.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Promoter enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP in ripening fruit
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry from phenylalanine to phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate, initiating general phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies entry metabolite for all downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct MYB12 target supporting increased general phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL5A promoter was significantly enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP. 2. It is one of the direct secondary-metabolism targets through which AtMYB12 enhances phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit at 3–4 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three PAL5 isoforms specifically highlighted.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Promoter enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP in ripening fruit
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry from phenylalanine to phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate, initiating general phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies entry metabolite for all downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct MYB12 target supporting increased general phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL5C promoter was significantly enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP. 2. It is one of the direct secondary-metabolism targets through which AtMYB12 enhances phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit at 3–4 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three PAL5 isoforms specifically highlighted.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Promoter enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP in ripening fruit
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry from phenylalanine to phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate, initiating general phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies entry metabolite for all downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct MYB12 target supporting increased general phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL5D promoter was significantly enriched in AtMYB12 ChIP. 2. It is one of the direct secondary-metabolism targets through which AtMYB12 enhances phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit at 3–4 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three PAL5 isoforms specifically highlighted.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in transgenic tomato fruit line 35S:VvStSy; combined with E8:AtMYB12 by crossing
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Resveratrol branch diverging from flavonoid precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form resveratrol, diverting flux from chalcone/flavonoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Resveratrol; polydatin; resveratrol glycosides; methylated resveratrol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexin and bioactive compound with reported therapeutic potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ~0.5 mg g^-1 DW trans-resveratrol alone, and 5–6 mg g^-1 DW total resveratrol derivatives when co-expressed with AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme strongly competing with CHS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; genetic crossing with AtMYB12; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 35S:VvStSy tomato fruit accumulated about 0.5 mg g^-1 DW trans-resveratrol. 2. Co-expression with AtMYB12 increased total resveratrol compounds to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW, mainly via increased polydatin. 3. High flavonol levels seen in AtMYB12 fruit were suppressed in AtMYB12 StSy fruit, indicating strong competition of StSy with CHS.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit harvested about 7–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redirects flux away from flavonols by competing effectively with CHS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to gene as StSy from grape and transgene line as 35S:VvStSy.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Fruit-specific transgene expression in tomato under E8 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanone substrate into isoflavone product, enabling genistein biosynthesis in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogenic isoflavone with proposed health effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LjIFS alone produced low genistin (~0.3 mg g^-1 DW), AtMYB12 increased genistin to ~11 mg g^-1 DW, and combining AtMYB12 with are mutation raised total isoflavones to ~78 mg g^-1 DW
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme whose output is constrained by competition with F3H
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; gateway construct with E8 promoter; genetic crossing with AtMYB12 and are; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E8:LjIFS expression enabled isoflavone production in tomato fruit, mainly as genistin. 2. AtMYB12 strongly enhanced LjIFS-derived isoflavone accumulation by increasing precursor supply. 3. Reducing competing F3H activity through are mutation further boosted total isoflavones to ~78 mg g^-1 DW.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit-specific expression in ripening tomato; are mutant background affects outcome
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Output depends on competition with F3H for naringenin; reducing F3H lowers flavonols while increasing isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper measured mainly glycosylated forms; genistin was principal isoflavonoid detected in LjIFS-only fruit.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Fruit-specific transgene expression in ripening fruit/pericarp driven by E8 promoter; strongly induced at 3–4 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; chlorogenic acid/caffeoylquinic acids; anthocyanins (when combined with Del/Ros1); resveratrol derivatives; genistin/isoflavones
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phytochemicals with proposed human health benefits; antioxidant/phytonutrient compounds
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis; precursor supply; branch support; carbon flux reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening via E8 promoter context
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlENO; SlDAHPS; PAL5A; PAL5C; PAL5D; SlCHS1; SlF3H; additional probable targets PGLS; Rpi; ALDO; SUS1; DHQD; SHD; PAT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of genes in glycolysis, oxidative pentose phosphate pathway, shikimate pathway and early flavonoid/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding to TACCTACC motif
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward-like activation of precursor supply and downstream specialized metabolism; possible metabolite repression in photosynthetic tissues discussed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes accumulation of flavonols and hydroxycinnamates in tomato fruit and boosts production of anthocyanins, resveratrol derivatives, and isoflavones when combined with additional genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator enabling unusually high phenylpropanoid production by coordinating supply and demand
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; promoter scanning; qRT-PCR; 14C/13C glucose feeding flux analysis; metabolite profiling; transgenic expression; genetic crosses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB12 upregulated genes of primary metabolism together with flavonoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. It directly bound promoters of SlENO, SlDAHPS, PAL5A/C/D, SlCHS1 and SlF3H through a conserved TACCTACC motif. 3. It increased carbon flux through glycolysis, OPPP and TCA cycle, raising aromatic amino acid supply and reducing sugars. 4. Co-expression with Del/Ros1, VvStSy or LjIFS markedly enhanced anthocyanin, stilbene and isoflavone accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit sampled around 3–10 days after breaker; fruit-specific expression context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced growth observed in tobacco when expressed from CaMV 35S promoter; major sugars decreased in tomato fruit; constitutive expression considered less effective due to metabolite repression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High value for biofortification and metabolic engineering of phenylpropanoids and specialty phytonutrients in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene source species is Arabidopsis, but effects were studied in tomato. AtMYB12 acts on both primary and secondary metabolism, unlike Del/Ros1 which mainly affect demand.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly expressed in fruit peel, not flesh, at 5 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols and related phenylpropanoids in tomato peel
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit tissue specificity/developmental state in peel during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlENO; SlDAHPS; SlCHS1; also associated with SlF3H and SlFLS expression in peel
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of primary-metabolism and flavonol-biosynthetic genes in peel
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding to the same motifs recognized by AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with elevated expression of genes involved in both primary and secondary metabolism in peel and likely promotes peel flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional endogenous homolog sharing target genes with AtMYB12 in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in peel vs flesh; ChIP-qPCR promoter scanning in WT peel; immunodetection after E. coli expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 is mainly expressed in tomato peel rather than flesh. 2. SlMYB12 binds promoters of SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 at the same motifs as AtMYB12. 3. Its expression is associated with higher transcript levels of SlENO, SlDAHPS, SlCHS1, SlF3H and SlFLS in peel.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: WT fruit peel and flesh sampled at 5 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential endogenous target for improving peel flavonoids and phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as endogenous tomato functional homolog of AtMYB12; not expressed in tomato flesh according to paper.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in purple Del/Ros1 tomato fruit line
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes encoding enzymes late in flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression, mainly late pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit; with AtMYB12 gives Indigo fruit with further increased anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Anthocyanin-promoting regulator but less effective than AtMYB12 for broad phenylpropanoid enhancement because it does not activate primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic cross with AtMYB12; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of anthocyanins/flavonols/CGA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del, together with Ros1, activated anthocyanin biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly upregulated genes late in flavonoid biosynthesis and did not significantly activate primary metabolism. 3. Combination with AtMYB12 further increased anthocyanins and other phenylpropanoids in Indigo fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit around 7 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Aromatic amino acids did not increase in Del/Ros1 fruit unlike AtMYB12 fruit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for anthocyanin biofortification, especially combined with AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of Del/Ros1 purple tomato line; source species inferred from known transgenic line, not explicitly detailed here.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in purple Del/Ros1 tomato fruit line
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes encoding enzymes late in flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression, mainly late pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit; with AtMYB12 contributes to Indigo fruit phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Anthocyanin-promoting regulator acting with Del; does not reprogram primary metabolism like AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic cross with AtMYB12; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of anthocyanins/flavonols/CGA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ros1, together with Del, activated anthocyanin biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly upregulated late flavonoid pathway genes and did not significantly induce primary metabolism. 3. In combination with AtMYB12, anthocyanin and total phenylpropanoid levels were further enhanced.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening fruit around 7 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Aromatic amino acids did not increase in Del/Ros1 fruit unlike AtMYB12 fruit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for anthocyanin biofortification, especially combined with AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of Del/Ros1 purple tomato line; source species inferred from known transgenic line, not explicitly detailed here.

Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato
